```python
import re

class JobDescriptionParser:
    def __init__(self, job_description):
        self.job_description = job_description
        self.requirements = {}

    def parse_job_title(self):
        # Attempt to extract job title from the beginning of the description
        lines = self.job_description.split('\n')
        if lines:
            job_title = lines[0].strip()
            if job_title:
                self.requirements['job_title'] = job_title
                return

        # If not found at the beginning, use a regex
        match = re.search(r"(?:Job Title:|Title:)\s*([A-Za-z\s]+)", self.job_description)
        if match:
            self.requirements['job_title'] = match.group(1).strip()
        else:
            self.requirements['job_title'] = "Job Title Not Found"

    def parse_skills(self):
        # Look for common phrases indicating skill lists
        skill_indicators = [
            r"(Skills:|Required Skills:|Technical Skills:|Skills Required:)",
            r"(Experience:|Required Experience:)",
            r"Proficiency in",
            r"Knowledge of"
        ]

        skills = []
        for indicator in skill_indicators:
            match = re.search(indicator, self.job_description, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                start_index = match.end()
                # Attempt to find the end of the skills section using keywords like "Responsibilities", "Qualifications"
                end_indicators = [r"(Responsibilities:|Qualifications:|Duties:)", r"(And|Or)"]
                end_index = len(self.job_description)  # Default to the end of the document
                for end_indicator in end_indicators:
                    end_match = re.search(end_indicator, self.job_description[start_index:], re.IGNORECASE)
                    if end_match:
                        end_index = start_index + end_match.start()
                        break
                skill_section = self.job_description[start_index:end_index].strip()

                # Split the skill section into individual skills based on common separators
                skill_list = re.split(r"[,;\n•]+", skill_section)
                for skill in skill_list:
                    skill = skill.strip()
                    if skill:
                        skills.append(skill)
                break  # Stop after finding the first skill section

        self.requirements['skills'] = skills

    def parse_experience(self):
        match = re.search(r"(\d+)\+?\s*(years|year)\s+experience", self.job_description, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            self.requirements['experience'] = int(match.group(1))
        else:
            self.requirements['experience'] = "Experience Not Specified"

    def parse_education(self):
        education_indicators = [
            r"(Education:|Qualifications:)",
            r"Bachelor's Degree",
            r"Master's Degree",
            r"Ph\.D\."
        ]

        education = []
        for indicator in education_indicators:
            match = re.search(indicator, self.job_description, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                start_index = match.end()
                 # Attempt to find the end of the education section using keywords like "Skills", "Responsibilities"
                end_indicators = [r"(Skills:|Responsibilities:|Experience:)", r"(And|Or)"]
                end_index = len(self.job_description)  # Default to the end of the document
                for end_indicator in end_indicators:
                    end_match = re.search(end_indicator, self.job_description[start_index:], re.IGNORECASE)
                    if end_match:
                        end_index = start_index + end_match.start()
                        break

                education_section = self.job_description[start_index:end_index].strip()
                education_list = re.split(r"[,;\n•]+", education_section)
                for edu in education_list:
                    edu = edu.strip()
                    if edu:
                        education.append(edu)
                break

        self.requirements['education'] = education

    def parse_responsibilities(self):
        responsibility_indicators = [
            r"(Responsibilities:|Duties:)"
        ]
        responsibilities = []
        for indicator in responsibility_indicators:
            match = re.search(indicator, self.job_description, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                start_index = match.end()
                end_indicators = [r"(Skills:|Qualifications:|Experience:)", r"(And|Or)"]
                end_index = len(self.job_description)
                for end_indicator in end_indicators:
                    end_match = re.search(end_indicator, self.job_description[start_index:], re.IGNORECASE)
                    if end_match:
                        end_index = start_index + end_match.start()
                        break
                responsibility_section = self.job_description[start_index:end_index].strip()
                responsibility_list = re.split(r"[,;\n•]+", responsibility_section)
                for resp in responsibility_list:
                    resp = resp.strip()
                    if resp:
                        responsibilities.append(resp)
                break

        self.requirements['responsibilities'] = responsibilities
    def parse(self):
        self.parse_job_title()
        self.parse_skills()
        self.parse_experience()
        self.parse_education()
        self.parse_responsibilities()
        return self.requirements

# Example usage (replace with your actual job description)
if __name__ == '__main__':
    job_description_text = """
Job Title: Software Engineer

Skills:
• Python
• JavaScript
• SQL
• AWS

Experience:
5+ years experience

Education:
Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science

Responsibilities:
• Develop and maintain web applications
• Write clean, testable code
• Collaborate with other engineers
"""

    parser = JobDescriptionParser(job_description_text)
    parsed_requirements = parser.parse()

    print(parsed_requirements)
```
